title: How to assign entries in same BJCP category to different flights
author: price95682
body: In an upcoming competition there are two groups of entrants that have entries in the same BJCP category but need to be in separate flights.  Google queries say this is possible.  We are using BCOE&M version 3.0.0 on our own server, not hosted.  I have set "Use Queued Judging" to Off.  However, the available documentation and query results don't say details of how to assign the entrants to separate flights.  How can this be done?

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body: A few of things to check. The first you already mentioned - setting "Use Queued Judging" to off via Judging Competition/Organization preferences. The second is what the Maximum Entries per Flight number is on the same preferences screen - this is the maximum number of entries a judge pair will be assigned to evaluate in the system per flight. Generally, this number is 12 or under. Third is what the Maximum Rounds per Session number is, again on the same preferences screen - this is the maximum number of judging rounds for each defined judging session. Fourth, check how many rounds each of your Judging Sessions has defined by going to Admin Dashboard > Preparing > Manage Judging Sessions. You can define rounds for each session up to the Maximum Rounds per Session you checked earlier. If you wish to have a judging session feature multiple rounds, this is where to delineate that. Most comps I've seen simply have a single round for each judging session. However, the ability is there if you need it. Next, go to Admin Dashboard > Organizing > Manage Tables. The steps to create and assign judges and stewards are laid out for you there: First, make sure to assign judges and stewards to their respective "pools." As of version 3.0.0, judges and stewards are automatically assigned to their respective pools upon registration _providing they registered as one or the other_. Next, define/create your table. 1. Select the Add a Table button or link from the "Step 2: Define All Tables" section. 2. Create your by Table by giving it a name, a number, assigning it to a judging session, and selecting one or more sub-categories to include. 3. Select Add 4. To be able to split the table into 2 or more flights, you need to make sure that the number of entries **exceeds** the Maximum Entries per Flight (red arrow below). Then, define your flights. Again, you'll only be able to split the table into flights if the number of entries at the table exceeds the Maximum Entries per Flight. 1. Expand the "Step 3: Define All Flights" section. 2. Select the table from the drop-down 3. Select the corresponding radio buttons to assign each entry to a flight. 4. Select Update After defining flights, you'll need to assign each flight to a round in the judging session. If your Maximum Rounds per Session is 1, you can skip this step. Otherwise, expand "Step 4: Assign Flights to Rounds" and select assign each flight to a round. After that, you can then assign judges to flights at each table. Hope that helps!

title: Version 2.8.2: Can't add judges to tables
author: ecomeaux
body:
Searched Issue Reports for Bug? No

Reviewed the Good To Know Issue Reports? No

- Version: 2.8.2
- Installation URL: https://brewingcompetitions.com/bluffcitybrewers/
- Hosted Installation? No

**Describe the Bug**

Can't add judges to tables, in either planning mode or preparation mode. Was able to add some judges to Session 1, but have repeatedly tried to add judges to remaining tables in subsequent sessions. We need to assign judges like now. It allows me to go to assign judges, click the available judges to assign to the table, then click Assign Judges, and then says Info edited successfully, but then no judges are actually assigned, it remains empty. Tried many times & refreshed & whatnot. Why can't I assign judges to my tables?

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
Hi @ecomeaux - I'm sorry you're experiencing this issue. However, but I'm not able to recreate it in my 2.8.2 test environment. I created multiple tables and was able to assign/unassign judges and stewards to each in both Tables Planning and Competition modes. 

It's curious that you were able to assign judges to Session 1, but not to others. This indicates that the assignment processing scripting worked at that time, so there may be other conditions that were/are present when you're attempting to add judges to other tables assigned to other sessions. 

If you are able to pull your server logs from the time when the failure occurred, those could help to pinpoint any processing errors.

Could you provide some more insight into your environment and setup, specifically:
- Whether or not you are still able to assign/unassign judges to tables that are in Session 1
- All available judges are assigned to the "judge pool" - I'm assuming yes since you're seeing them when you try to assign to tables.
- If you're not using queued judging, make sure all tables have flights defined
- If you're not using queued judging, make sure all flights are assigned to rounds
- Whether or not you have added any sessions after the judging signup window (judges who signed up before any sessions were added will not be marked as "available" for them)
- Whether or not you have any mods or altered code running

comments.author: ecomeaux
comments.body: AH yes that was it, the Tables had not yet been assigned to rounds. Now I am able to assign judges. Thank you for the quick response!

title: Home Page SQL Error - bootstrap.php is Being False-Flagged by Immunify 360 (Web Host Security)
author: NCBeerGuys
body:
I'm getting this message on the site - no idea how or why it was created.

You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MariaDB server version for the right syntax to use near '' at line 1

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
This is an issue that came about this week - apparently, the /site/bootstrap.php file is being false-flagged by Immunify as containing malware and subsequently scrubbed. 

To rectify, you'll need to restore the bootstrap.php file in your BCOEM directory structure and then make sure that the file is on your web host's Immunify "exceptions" list so it won't be scrubbed again.

I'm currently on vacation, but will be looking into why this is happening when I return.

comments.body:
Thanks for the reply - the site is back up after I uploaded the backup of the bootstap.php file. I have no idea where the host's Immunify "exceptions" list is - I'm assuming I would have to contact the host or if it's a file I can update? It can wait until you return - enjoy your vacation!

Cheers!

comments.body:
I found this help article from Immunify360 - if one of you would, please follow that procedure to submit the file as a triggering a false-positive - none of the sites I maintain have Immunify360. When you report, please mention that the file is part of an open-source project maintained at GitHub here https://github.com/geoffhumphrey/brewcompetitiononlineentry.

https://docs.imunify360.com/patchman/frequently_asked_questions/#how-do-i-report-an-incorrect-detection-false-positive

From the help file:

> If you believe that a detection is a false positive, please follow the following steps to report this to us:
>
> Get a copy of the exact file on your website that is flagged for detection by Patchman. Make note of the affected website, which server it is detected on, and the full file path of that file.
>
> Send an email to [support@patchman.co](mailto:support@patchman.co) in which you mention all the above details, and include the exact file as an attachment. **Please do not copy-paste the file’s contents into the email body. Some data may be lost which slows down our ability to help you. It must be included as an attachment.**
>
> Based on all this information, we will investigate the detection. If it is considered a legitimate detection, we will explain why that is. If it is indeed an incorrect detection, we will retract the signature, which would lead to automatically retracting all detections based on that signature.

title: Enhancement: Cup Labels
author: kmeintsma
body:
Some competitions have moved to a double blind format for judging.

To make this possible and reduce errors, having the entry information printed on a label that can be put on the cups is very helpful.

The current software version only permits this for BOS.

If it could be added for normal flights/rounds and mini-bos as well it would be a big help.

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
Hi @kmeintsma,

Labels for all entries can be created now by using downloaded data and mail merging into [MS Word](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/use-mail-merge-for-bulk-email-letters-labels-and-envelopes-f488ed5b-b849-4c11-9cff-932c49474705) or [Google Docs](https://www.gmass.co/blog/mail-merge-google-docs/).

First go to Admin > Data Exports > Entries and Associated Data > All Entries: Limited Data CSV in your BCOEM installation.

Open the CSV in Excel or Google Sheets and remove all of the extraneous data, [like in this example](https://github.com/geoffhumphrey/brewcompetitiononlineentry/files/9668048/Example_BCOEM_Entries_Mail_Merge.csv), leaving the entry or judging number (depending upon what you're using), category, and required info in place. Then, you could sort by category number, save as a CSV, and then perform a Mail Merge in MS Word or Google Docs to produce labels that can be adhered to individual cups.

comments.body:
While it's correct that labels can be created through a data dump and mail merge, the idea for this enhancement is to reduce the workload and decrease the time it takes to get things done. This is particularly important during mini-bos and bos activities because you can't prepare for this in advance.

Having the option just to generate a list of the mini-bos entries by category would be a big help.

If I'm the only customer that want's the enhancement it's probably not worthwhile. I'll keep sending suggestions as I run into problems to be solved.

title: Version 2.4.0(e) - Splitting a Sub-Style at Tables; Adding Judging Sessions After Registration Begins
author: kmeintsma
body:
I have a more detailed question and can't find anything in the software or in the help system to answer it.

Question 1:
I have a large category (multiple actually) that I want to split into two categories by sweetness for both judging and awards. How can I do this?

It's simple enough to create flights for a table that would be separated by sweetness if I look at the entry information although it's extra work. But awards are reported by table correct? I can't create extra tables as far as I can tell and then assign flights to them based on the characteristics I'm targeting.

Question 2:
I've added some additional judging sessions. When I try to assign judges to a table by session, the software will NOT allow me to select the judges I need. I presume it's because they didn't sign up for the session due to it being created after signups were done. Is there a way around this? I really don't want to go into each judge profile and force select them into a new judging session.

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
Hi @kmeintsma - hopefully the following will answer your questions.

**Question 1**
You can spilt into flights like you described. And yes, awards are by table. If you want to have awards for the differing levels of sweetness within a sub-category, my advice would be to:
1. Create a custom "style" for each level of sweetness.
2. Reassign the affected entries to the appropriate custom style.
3. Create a judging table for each level of sweetness.

This way, you'll have medal categories for each.

**Question 2**
Correct. There are a couple of approaches around this.
1. Send a mass email to all judges informing of the newly created judging session(s) and requesting that they update their availability via the Edit My Account function.
2. Just like you mentioned - edit the accounts of those judges whom you know are available yourself as the Admin. 

Not allowing judges to be assigned to sessions where they're explicitly unavailable is a failsafe in the system.

Cheers!

comments.author: kmeintsma
comments.body:
The difficulty with creating a custom style AFTER all entries are in the system is that there is no good way to reassign the desired entries.  *If there is, I would like to hear about it.*

To the best of my knowledge it requires me to go into each person's profile and explicitly move the entry to the new category/style. When I have tried this in the past, it ALSO removed the entry name, all of the carb/sweetness and optional information. This is not a practical approach with 400 entries.

Mead competitions from an informational/category standpoint are very different from beer. We have all sorts or information that needs to stay with the entry, and we need that information available to the judges as well. With beer, it's pretty straight forward, there are few substyles that have optional/added ingredients.

The comps that are well mananged are gradually moving to a totally blind presentation. In this case, we have stewards pouring for the judges and delivering cups to the judging table. This permits us (again especially for mead) to accept odd bottle formats. To facilitate this approach and minimize mistakes, we put labels on the cups - similar to the BOS labels
you generate in the system. Sadly, this facility is not available for anything other than BOS. Having placemats available would be a nice addition as well.

I've dumped and massaged the data, and I've written a script to concatenate the data into a label format that can be printed, but it would be great to have the system provide labels as needed. Something for the future perhaps.

Adding judges to a new session is time consuming, but there are few enough of them that it's reasonably easy to do.

I have a list of other issues/bugs we've encountered and I'll send them along after the competition is finished.

Thanks!
Kevin

When the style is changed to the new style, ALL of the data is wiped out.

Thank you!

For enhancements, please prepend "Enhancement - " to the title. For issues/bugs, please prepend the BCOE&M version number the title.

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
Hi Jim - no, this isn't a problem. If your entry window is still open, I would hold off trying to plan your tables configurations until it closes. 

If that isn't possible, or if you'd like to get a jump on things, there's no harm in planning your tables while actively accepting entries - the only risk is that your entry counts per table may be inaccurate. If you do go that route, my advice would be edit _each_ table _after_ your entry window closes to make sure you've accurately captured all entry styles and their associated counts. 

Once you've sorted and marked entries as received, you can then switch back to Tables Competition Mode. To go back to Tables Competition Mode, go to Admin > Organizing > Manage Tables. Then, simply select the "Switch to Tables Competition Mode" button near the top.

comments.author: geoffhumphrey

Hi @cgspeck, if you'd like to make the _All with Scores_ report available to all entrants, you could access that report and print it to PDF via Chrome or other browser. Then, using the scoresheet upload feature, upload it to your installation's /user_docs/ (or upload it to another directory on your server, Google Docs, Dropbox, etc.). Then, you could employ a simple HTML custom module. For example, you could customize the code in the /mods/sidebar_top_example.php file to be something like: ```

Full Results List

``` Save it as a PHP file, upload it to the /mods/ directory of your BCOE&M installation, and then add it as a custom module by going to Admin > Preferences/Customization > Custom Modules > Add. title: Enhancement: Load AABC 2022 guidelines into current prod version
author: rocket-pants
body:
**Is your enhancement or feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Running Australian comps this years needs to align with the new AABC guidelines. I'm not keen on upgrading to 2.5 yet given the beta status.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Load AABC 2022 guidelines into v2.4 (or 2.4.1 I guess) or provide the steps to do so via phpmyadmin

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Custom categories and styles don't really work that well when adding a large number of new styles

**Additional context**
Please help :)

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
Hi @rocket-pants - try the following to integrate the 2022 AABC styles into version 2.4.0:
- Download this archive and unzip it: [v240_AABC2022.zip](https://github.com/geoffhumphrey/brewcompetitiononlineentry/files/9137156/v240_AABC2022.zip)
- Using phpMyAdmin, import the **AABC2022_styles.sql** file into your BCOE&M database
  - **If you use a prefix for your tables**, make sure to change the `style` table name in the INSERT INTO statement on line 14 of the file to incorporate your prefix, like so...
`` INSERT INTO `prefix_styles` ``
- Upload the **styles.inc.php** file in the zip to your BCOE&M installation's **/includes/ directory** - replace the one that is there.
- Fire up your BCOE&M installation, log in as an Admin, and:
  - Go to Admin > Preferences > Site Preferences and change your Style Set to AABC 2022
  - Go to Admin > Competition Preparation > Styles Accepted and select all of the styles you'd like to accept for your competition.

Of course, please do so in a test environment prior to deploying on a production installation. 

Please note, I have NOT tested this as yet with any other functions other than adding/editing entries. Off the top of my head, the functions that would be most impacted are creating and editing Tables (Admin > Organizing > Tables > Manage), some reports, and data exports.

Comment here if you have any issues.

A couple of other little things

title: Informational: PayPal IPN Emails
author: jimmyorkin
body:
Version: 2.1.13


Installation URL:


Is your installation hosted on brewcompetition.com or brewcomp.com? No


Description of Issue: I saw a strange error with our email deliver provider, Mailgun, where emails were failing to be delivered. After investigation this is what I found.

Our Club uses PayPal for BCOEM and to take member dues and raffle money, IPN sends notifications for any PayPal event and BCOEM sees them. In this case our treasurer was taking dues payments. When the IPN message got to BCOEM, which is displaying the results of the last competition state, BCOEM tried to send emails about the transactions. these emails were not deliverable.

We probably should turn off IPN in the off season. I don't know if any fix is needed in BCOEM. Something to be aware of.

The BCOEM version is old. It will be updated before the next competition.

Jimmy

title: Can I use "Competition Categories" at tables instead of styles?
author: mlk2b
body:
We have a fairly small competition (about 100 entries) and tend to group various styles into Competition Categories. (For example, the Dark Standard category has American Stouts compete with British Porters. The Light Standard category has Czech Pils compete against Maibocks.) In my experience, though, the software only allows one style per table, which isn't feasible in our small competition. Is there a simple way around this so that I can assign multiple styles to one table for judging? 

Here's our site from last year's competition. http://throwdown.starcitybrewers.org/

Thanks for any help you can offer! (I'm pretty new to github and BCOEM so I apologize if this is a newb question!)

comments.author: ercousin
comments.body:
What do you mean by "one style per table"? Do you mean style category? Even so, that isn't true, you can group whichever style into whichever table you want using the "Add a Table" step.

Eg.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1551339/42414454-ad726fe2-8203-11e8-9e10-65f0225eb2c8.png)

The installation I'm pasting from already has all tables created, so ignore the red highlighting, but you should see something like this.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1551339/42414470-dd20d1d4-8203-11e8-8e9d-4695b757a2a2.png)

comments.author: mlk2b
comments.body:
I might be a tad confused. Thanks for bearing with me. 

I think our biggest issues come with beers like Spice, Herb, and Vegetable beers. Since they're all 30A, the software doesn't like me sending them to different tables. For example, can I send a Vanilla Porter (30A) to a "Dark Specialty" table and a Mango IPA (30A) to a "Light Specialty" table? My understanding is I have to assign the whole category to a single table.

comments.author: ercousin
comments.body:
Correct, whole subcategory like 30A must be judged together as far as BCOEM is concerned. The Req Info specified by the entrant is just a text field. There's nothing to stop you from doing this manually, but then you wouldn't be able to report results through BCOEM.

Also, Mango IPA would be 29A Fruit Beer, since it's a fruited version of a classic style.

IMO it's fairer to entrants to separate Specialty by established subcategory than try and interpret entrant intent and create "light specialty" and "dark specialty".
Eg.
Table 5 - American Wild Ale
Table 6 - Fruit Beer
Table 7 - SHV Beer
Table 8 - Other Specialty Beer

comments.author: mlk2b
comments.body:
Okay, I think I understand the situation a little better now. Thank you.

My understanding is that it's currently not possible to assign individual entries to tables (you have to assign the whole subcategory). How difficult would it be to develop a version of BCOEM with the option of assigning individual entries to tables, rather than whole subcategories? Thanks again, guys!

comments.author: geoffhumphrey
comments.body:
Yes, as it stands in all versions, the entire category of 30A would have to be "lumped" together in any given comp. Logistically, for me personally as a organizer, this is certainly sufficiently granular. However, if you want to lump certain entries together, you can do that too when you define the flights at a given table. Just make sure that you adjust your comp's judging preferences to NOT utilize the queued method. Then, define your table and then define each flight at that table, placing like entries in the same flight.

Judges are certainly used to SHVs being combined and it's the table/flight's Head Judge's responsibility to order the pulling of entries (especially so if queued judging is being utilized); as a result, a natural division will occur based upon what the entrant has declared. A competent HJ would not (and should not) lump dissimilar entries together unless absolutely necessary.

title: Feature request: Print barcoded judging numbers per table
author: markalston
body:
I want the ability to print judging/entry number barcode labels per table.

The ability to scan and upload scoresheets is quite useful. However, naming and merging the scanned files for a huge number of scoresheets will be quite cumbersome.  

There exists software which can find a barcode on a tiff/pdf file and rename/merge/split it based on the barcode.  If barcodes are added to each scoresheet I can utilize this software to automatically name the pdfs for uploading.  

I am planning on using external software this weekend (glables and libreoffice) to print barcoded labels for each scoresheet.   I am going to have the judges apply them on the top of each scoresheet.  Then I can simply bulk scan in the scoresheets and not worry about manually naming them.

In the future, I can imagine adding this ability to our web server so that I can simply upload randomly named tiff/pdf files and then the server will rename them based on the barcode.

comments.author: jimmyorkin
comments.body:
I was planning on doing something very similar. I was thinking about printing the scoresheets on demand as judges took flights. The scoresheet would have the judge and entry information and barcode printed.

Then as we get flights back we could drop all the competed scoresheets in the scanner and print one file per scoresheet. Then have the barcode reading software read the judging number barcode and rename the file based on the judging number.

I am very interested in this effort and am willing to help.

comments.author: markalston
comments.body:
I think printing scoresheets on demand will be a bit slower than you think. At least for our comp of 400+ entries it would be challenging. 

Anyway, here is what I have learned and implemented today during day one of our comp.

1) Scan in the scoresheets in bulk. Doesn't matter what order but make sure each batch has all the sheets for those entries.  You will be generating very large multipage tiff files.
 
2) When scanning do so to tiff files with 300 dpi resolution and grayscale colorspace.  (Tried black and white first due to suggestions on the net but zbar failed miserably in finding the codes).  (I used windows fax and scan on my brother MFC printer).

3) Upload these files to your server.

4) Use my bash script below to extract each scoresheet, get the qr code from a blurred crop of the tiff (blurring yielded much much better qr code extraction for some reason and cropping sped up the qr detection), sort the scoresheets by code and then finally generate multipage pdfs from those tifs.

Bash Script:
```
#!/bin/bash
# Bash script to auto sort and generate pdfs from scanned scoresheets
# Author: Mark Alston
#
# First change the IFS in case our filenames have spaced
SAVEIFS=$IFS
IFS=$'\n'
# Generate the necessary folders but don't complain if they exist.
mkdir -p processed
mkdir -p split
mkdir -p blurred
mkdir -p entries
mkdir -p pdfs

for file in *.tif
do
#  Figure out how many scoresheets are in the file.
    PAGES=$(identify ${file} | wc -l)

    for ((i=0;i<$PAGES;i++))
    do
        echo "$file $i"

#      extract the top of the scoresheet and blur it for qr code detection. Move it into the blurred subfolder.
        convert ${file}[$i] -scene 1 -blur 0 -extract 1240x400 blurred/${file%.tif}_$i.tif

#      extract the scoresheet with normal resolution and move it into a temp folder.
        convert ${file}[$i] -scene 1 split/${file%.tif}_$i.tif

#      Find the entry number in the scoresheet and convert to 6 digits.
        ENTRY=$(zbarimg  -Si25.disable -q --raw blurred/${file%.tif}_$i.tif)
        ENTRY=`printf %06d $ENTRY`

#      Move the scoresheet into a separate entry folder 
#         Note: If the entry number wasn't found it will simply leave it in the main entries folder.
#                   You will then need to manually go through those to find the number.  
#                   I have had very few of these.  8 out of hundreds didn't scan.
        mkdir -p entries/$ENTRY
        mv split/${file%.tif}_$i.tif entries/$ENTRY

    done
    cd entries
    for ENTRY in `find * -type d`
    do
        # Make the final pdf from the tiffs and move a pdf folder.
        convert ${ENTRY}/*.tif ../pdfs/${ENTRY}.pdf
        rm -rf ${ENTRY}
    done
    cd ..
    mv ${file} processed
done
IFS=$SAVEIFS
```

comments.author: markalston
comments.body:
one final note  you can run ps2pdf on the final pdfs to greatly reduce their size without much if any reduction in quality.

We did this to copy the pdfs into the final folder

```
for file in *.pdf
do
ps2pdf $file ../user_docs/$file
done
```

comments.author: markalston
comments.body:
Final note after using this method.  We will add the full subcategory name to the labels next year as brewers were somewhat confused figuring out which of their beers the scoresheets belonged to since the judging numbers were different from their entry numbers.  We did include the sheet showing the correlation for each into their packets but still, you know brewers.

Overall though it worked great and was very easy to implement (once I figured out the bash script above).

comments.author: jimmyorkin
comments.body:
I want to give an update on what we have done with the Bluebonnet Brewoff in 2018. I wrote some php pages that use fpdf and fpdi to generate PDF files of the scoresheets. The scoresheets have the barcode I need for managing the scoresheets for return to the entrants. It also reaches into the database and pulls all the information about the entry and prints them on the scoresheet for the judges. It also prints the judge information. The page supports beer, mead and cider scoresheets. A sample is attached.

We judged 1379 entries with 1st round scoresheets.

I had the judges sit down and enter their information directly so any mistakes was on them. The judges printed their own scoresheets right before they sat down to judge a flight. We have a HP M521 printer/scanner that is pretty fast. We had no issues by printing the scoresheets as we went.

The zlibimg tool worked great. Thanks to Mark for his work in finding the tool. I did not have to blur the image. It worked fine just reading the tiff files.

There is still work to be done. What I had save hours of scanning time since I could scan in large batches. Actually I scanned by flight. I left the paper scoresheets in flight order. The judges liked having all the information pre-filled in for them. I had a couple of judges that could not write their comments on the correct scoresheets. They reprinted them and rewrote their comments or used a copier to make a correct looking sheet.

My pages are hacks at best and do not run under the BCOEM environment. If there is interest productionizing my work, I would be interested.

[doc.pdf](https://github.com/geoffhumphrey/brewcompetitiononlineentry/files/2362092/doc.pdf)

comments.author: ercousin
comments.body: Thanks for sharing @jimmyorkin that's pretty cool!

title: Nginx Rewrite Rules?
author: dmofot
body:
Does anybody have the rewrite rules to do pretty urls for nginx?  Specifically lines 10-13 in the [.htaccess](https://github.com/geoffhumphrey/brewcompetitiononlineentry/blob/589336010a0b895906ee23cc170f7f19464be7dc/.htaccess).

```
RewriteRule ^$ index.php [L]
RewriteRule ^([A-Za-z0-9-]+)?$ index.php?section=$1 [L]
RewriteRule ^([A-Za-z0-9-]+)/([A-Za-z0-9-]+)/?$ index.php?section=$1&go=$2 [L]
RewriteRule ^([A-Za-z0-9-]+)/([A-Za-z0-9-]+)/([A-Za-z0-9-]+)/?$ index.php?section=$1&go=$2&action=$3 [L]
```
